subject: (no subject)
body:
Darling JE!
I hope your life is trekking along well.

Even though I don't always get along with Dubai, I'm happy to be here now
that the financial job market is taking a beating in London and New York.

I'm starting to make peace with Dubai, probably because I don't think I'll
be here for that much longer. Long enough to weather out the subprime storm.


There are too many crap people here to stay. The women are all some sort of
hookers.

Much love,
Cecilia

*Report #62:* *Things are better, but girls in Dubai will always be hookers*
*How to get a less ancient PC at low-paid job*
I kept kicking my ancient PC a little every day. Finally it broke down so
 they gave me a less ancient one. Oh goodie, now I have one that is just a
little bigger than a dog-house.

*Driving in Dubai*
Is not as self-explanatory as one would think. Let's take a look at the
manual:

   1. SUV is flashing full-lights in your rear-view mirror:  Get out of
   my way, NOW!!!!
   2. Car / SUV flashing full-lights in in intersection can mean a)
   I WILL NOT let you go before me - OR -  b) I WILL let you go before me. If
   it's a SUV you may want to give way.

*Dress-code appropriate for low-paid job*
Since my job pays so crappy I've decided only to wear cheap clothes to work.
For those of you who know me, knows my Italian closet... so I had to dig
deep... I will only wear my 15 year old, black suit from some cheap Italian
brand called .... Benetton.

Unfortunately, I only have PINK shirts, which are far too expensive to wear
for such low-paid job, but what can a girl do? I make too little to buy a
AED 20 shirt at H&M which would've been the appropriate price-range for my
salary.

*Middle-Eastern - Asia Leadership Forum: Day 1*
MAC sponsored this event so I, along with K and M, had been assigned to
stand at the booth. It was not much to do so I got to know colleagues pretty
well:

The kids here may be Muslim and close to their families and what not, but
they lie like no Western kids would ever have the energy to do!

K is a member of the Sharjah royal family. He tells his mother that he goes
away on business every weekend. Every weekend he goes to Maritius, Maldives,
Europe or Las Vegas on his cousin's private jet, partying like a rockstar.

K has 73 speeding tickets and will have to go to jail for a week or two. He
will send his driver in his place.

Wears Rolex he inherited from his father. It's valued at +$600,000.

M tells his mother he's at a spa in Abu Dhabi when he's actually partying
his head off in London.

*Middle-Eastern - Asia Leadership Forum: Day 2*
More food.
Got to know new employee, a French girl, small as a Chihuaha. She hates
Dubai. Had as many bad experiences as I have.

*Medical care a la Dubai*
My Thai colleague got terrible pains. The doctors are telling her nothing is
wrong with her, BUT they can prescribe really strong painkillers.
She might have to go back to Thailand to be checked out there.

After my gynecology visit I bled like a pig. I haven't had that much pain
for over ten years. Muslim gyn did something really wrong when she examined
me.

*Note to self: Don't seek medical attention in Dubai. If you're not sick,
you will become sick as a dog.*

*Glamorous life*
People don't have as glamorous lives in Dubai as one would think. Nor do
they have good friends. Thurs afternoon everyone and their mother at work
asked me what I was doing this weekend because nobody had plans. Granted,
all the "people" asking were guys and they all ended up bailing on me anyway
because they got too drunk too early or some French hooker called them (this
is an entirely different report...).

*Boys in Dubai*
I take back that all guys are assholes in Dubai. We have some really great
ones in our office. I will of course never date any of the nice guys in
Dubai because I'm addicted to commitment-phobic, cheap assholes preferably
from really bad families.

*Decades party*
Went to a great asw party where you had to dress according to 70's, 80's
etc. Dubai can be fun! Partying is better here than anywhere else in the
world. People are not afraid to get down and dirty here. In New York it's
like: "Oh, don't get my Prada-dress dirty!"

Dance like it hurts
Love Like You Need Money
Work When People are Watching
Scott Adams

-- 
Cecilia Steen
Dubai Mobile +971 50 940 2062

Darling JE!
I hope your life is trekking along well.
 
Even though I don't always get along with Dubai, I'm happy to be here now that the financial job market is taking a beating in London and New York.
 
I'm starting to make peace with Dubai, probably because I don't think I'll be here for that much longer. Long enough to weather out the subprime storm.
 
There are too many crap people here to stay. The women are all some sort of hookers.
 
Much love,

Report #62: Things are better, but girls in Dubai will always be hookers
How to get a less ancient PC at low-paid job
I kept kicking my ancient PC a little every day. Finally it broke down so  they gave me a less ancient one. Oh goodie, now I have one that is just a little bigger than a dog-house.
 
Driving in Dubai
Is not as self-explanatory as one would think. Let's take a look at the manual:
  1. SUV is flashing full-lights in your rear-view mirror:  Get out of my way, NOW!!!!
  2. Car / SUV flashing full-lights in in intersection can mean a) I WILL NOT let you go before me - OR -  b) I WILL let you go before me. If it's a SUV you may want to give way.
Dress-code appropriate for low-paid job
Since my job pays so crappy I've decided only to wear cheap clothes to work. For those of you who know me, knows my Italian closet... so I had to dig deep... I will only wear my 15 year old, black suit from some cheap Italian brand called .... Benetton.
 
Unfortunately, I only have PINK shirts, which are far too expensive to wear for such low-paid job, but what can a girl do? I make too little to buy a AED 20 shirt at H&M which would've been the appropriate price-range for my salary.
 
Middle-Eastern - Asia Leadership Forum: Day 1
MAC sponsored this event so I, along with K and M, had been assigned to stand at the booth. It was not much to do so I got to know colleagues pretty well:
 
The kids here may be Muslim and close to their families and what not, but they lie like no Western kids would ever have the energy to do!
 
K is a member of the Sharjah royal family. He tells his mother that he goes away on business every weekend. Every weekend he goes to Maritius, Maldives, Europe or Las Vegas on his cousin's private jet, partying like a rockstar.
 
K has 73 speeding tickets and will have to go to jail for a week or two. He will send his driver in his place.
 
Wears Rolex he inherited from his father. It's valued at +$600,000.
 
M tells his mother he's at a spa in Abu Dhabi when he's actually partying his head off in London.
 
Middle-Eastern - Asia Leadership Forum: Day 2
More food. 
Got to know new employee, a French girl, small as a Chihuaha. She hates Dubai. Had as many bad experiences as I have.  
 
Medical care a la Dubai
My Thai colleague got terrible pains. The doctors are telling her nothing is wrong with her, BUT they can prescribe really strong painkillers.
She might have to go back to Thailand to be checked out there.
 
After my gynecology visit I bled like a pig. I haven't had that much pain for over ten years. Muslim gyn did something really wrong when she examined me.
 
Note to self: Don't seek medical attention in Dubai. If you're not sick, you will become sick as a dog.
 
Glamorous life
People don't have as glamorous lives in Dubai as one would think. Nor do they have good friends. Thurs afternoon everyone and their mother at work asked me what I was doing this weekend because nobody had plans. Granted, all the "people" asking were guys and they all ended up bailing on me anyway because they got too drunk too early or some French hooker called them (this is an entirely different report...).
 
Boys in Dubai
I take back that all guys are assholes in Dubai. We have some really great ones in our office. I will of course never date any of the nice guys in Dubai because I'm addicted to commitment-phobic, cheap assholes preferably from really bad families. 
 
Decades party
Went to a great asw party where you had to dress according to 70's, 80's etc. Dubai can be fun! Partying is better here than anywhere else in the world. People are not afraid to get down and dirty here. In New York it's like: "Oh, don't get my Prada-dress dirty!"
 
Dance like it hurts
Love Like You Need Money
Work When People are Watching
Scott Adams
 
--
Cecilia Steen
